projects.title: Riverside Dental — digital intake
projects.tagline: Replaced a clipboard and a fax machine with a five-minute online intake.
projects.description: Riverside was printing ten-page intake packets for every new patient, then manually transcribing them into their records system. We shipped a tablet-friendly intake flow that saves the form directly into their existing software and flags incomplete submissions before the patient finishes checking in.
projects.outcome: New-patient intake dropped from 22 minutes to 4 minutes on average. Front-desk transcription time eliminated entirely.

projects.title: Oak & Co. Accounting — email triage
projects.tagline: An LLM assistant that sorts client emails, drafts routine replies, and flags anything it's unsure about.
projects.description: A small accounting firm was losing hours every week sorting through repetitive client inquiries. We built a triage assistant that reads incoming email, categorizes it, drafts a reply for the partner to review, and escalates anything ambiguous. Runs locally on the firm's own infrastructure — no client data leaves the building.
projects.outcome: Estimated 6–8 hours of partner time saved per week. Zero client data shared with third-party AI providers.

projects.title: Maple Studio — project dashboard
projects.tagline: One honest dashboard that replaced three spreadsheets and a whiteboard.
projects.description: The studio was tracking project status across spreadsheets, a Trello board, and someone's head. We built a single dashboard showing live project health, budget burn, and upcoming deadlines — pulling from their existing tools so nothing had to change about how they actually work.
projects.outcome: Monday planning meetings dropped from 90 minutes to 20. Partners finally agree on which project is behind schedule.
